feed.title: SAP Community - David Ruiz Badia
feed.subtitle: David Ruiz Badia in SAP Community

entry.title: SAP Inside Track Barcelona 2022 Recap
entry.content:
Hi all

After two years organizing the SAP Inside Track Barcelona event online, we were looking forward to  meeting again in person. This finally happened on Friday October 28th at the 6th edition of the SAP Inside Track Barcelona 2022 where more than 60 attendees including colleagues, collaborators, speakers, professionals and partners, dedicated a full day to learning the latest in SAP and had a chance to put into practice their skills at the SAP Code Jam session that took place in the afternoon.

Since our first SAP Inside Track Barcelona was created and promoted in 2017, the event’s organizing team has welcomed new members and more partners have become involved in promoting the event. The challenges of organizing an event like the SITBCN are many. Launching the call for papers to find speakers, coordinating the speakers, supervising the on-site requirements, coordinating the sponsors, publicizing the event and generating interest and putting together an interesting and varied agenda require forward planning and time dedication. All the hard work paid off in the end as the 6th edition of the SITBCN closed with a record number of attendees.

This year the event was shared with AUSAPE BTP Special Interest Group members as the

2022 October meeting.

This year agenda was divided into two sections. In the morning, the agenda included six plenary sessions whereas the afternoon took a practical approach through a SAP CodeJam on the topic of SAP Integration Suite.

Morning agenda:

9:05A Hitchhiker's Guide to SAP Fiori User Experience and its TechnologiesOliver Graeff
10:00Artificial Intelligence (AI) use in SAP processes - Available scenarios and scope (Use, Integrate, Develop)Antonio Leites-Lopez
10:35SAP - Inside Freelance experienceErnesto Ortega
11:30Development of FIORI Apps - Low code - No CodeDavid Bartolome
12:10Event based replication of data from S4 to BTPGregory Doukas
12:50Keytree a Deloitte Business - Digitally Transforming Brand Ambassadors with SAP BTP & SAP Cloud AnalyticsAnxo Ocampo
 

 

We would like to thank all our speakers for taking part in the event and sharing best practices and knowledge on the latest innovations regarding SAP BTP. Equally interesting was the presentation about the SAP freelance community and the research conducted on the different profiles.

Some of SAP Inside Track Barcelona attendees were kind enough to share with us their views on the event, here is one example:

 

After lunch, it was time to get into the SAP BTP CodeJam, an exercise about SAP Integration Suite and how we can use it to connect systems and services in our landscape. Run by Antonio Maradiaga, Developer Advocate, who explained the challenge to the participants and was available to solve their queries and questions. You can see all the Instructions to work in this CodeJam were posted here

Connecting Systems and Services Integration Suite CodeJam GitHub repo.

Also a SAP employee and SAP CodeJam BTP participant was happy to share his insights about the workshop:

An event like this would not be possible with the continued support from our sponsors.

We’d like to thank the following companies for helping us make the SITBCN a reality:

  • Books sponsors: SAP Press offered 6 free e-books and Espresso Tutorials a one year free subscription were drawn between all event attendees. Winners: Didac Sales, Leire Osa, Kontoulis Panagiotis, Carles Viaplana, Stefano Passaro, Jose Muñoz and Andrea Aymami.
  • Food sponsors:  The welcome breakfast and the lunch were sponsored by Malt and Deloitte. Beer was sponsored by Damm, fruit juices were sponsored by Granini and the snacks were provided by Frit Ravich.
  • Audio sponsors: Ausape and Neptune
  • Merchandising sponsor: Trifork covered the cost of the bags we offered to all our attendees.
  • Venue: Endesa allowed us to organize this event in their Modernist Room, an amazing space inside Endesa Headquarters in Barcelona.
  • Polo shirts: sponsored by Neptune
  • Overall event sponsor: SAP Community

 

 

It wasn’t all hard work, speakers and organizers enjoyed a pre-event dinner at the Restaurant La Pomarada. The SAP local community has been growing online in the past 2 years due to the pandemic conditions and this time, some of new SITBCN friends that regularly join the monthly stamstich meet-ups, registered to attend our event and it was great to meet them for the first time face to face. We shared good conversations and met new colleagues.

 

Last but not least, we would to invite to all of you to join the Barcelona Group, our recently created group at SAP Community, it is our objective to make it our regular meeting point to share SAP related information amongst Barcelona members.

The group has been created as a central point of information of this local community to share news, to generate debate and solve questions about any SAP related topics and to help SAP professionals network so that they can share SAP knowledge, case studies, or just cry together in the next SAP universal ID update 😊.

Needless to say, the group is open to anyone! So, help us grow by sharing it with all your SAP contacts.

 

And now, only to start to think to schedule next SAP Stammtisch Barcelona meetings to design next SAP Inside Track Barcelona 2023!

Looking forward to meeting you soon in any of our events/meetings, SAP Stammtisch Barcelona and/or SAP Inside Tracks around the globe. This SAP local community is growing, and we would like to growth with you ¡

Best

Enric, Lluis, Imma and David

SAP Inside Track Barcelona organizers

entry.title: Topics/Ideas for SAP Barcelona Community 2023
entry.content:
Hi all!

We are just closing 2022 and making some recap of what went well and what not.

With this reflexion in our minds, we would like to ask you about new ideas of activities to organize for next 2023.

We would like to have some SAP Barcelona stammtisch online and others in-person at Barcelona city center.

But also we could organize/attend/... any other format with your help, so any feedback will be more than welcome.

 

So, how do you imagine next 2023 SAP Barcelona Community life?

Waiting your feedback!

Best

And Merry Christams and Happy New Year !! 😉

#Barcelona #sitbarcelona #sapstammtischbarcelona @#sitbcn #sapstammtischbcn #sapcommunity

entry.title: Trip to USA east coast
entry.content:
Hi

Currently planning with my family, our summer holidays (July) to USA east coast (first time there).

Mainly, we would like to visit NY, Philadelphia, Niagara and Chicago.

I would like to request you any suggestion to visit there, what we could not miss, transfer and accomodation suggestions, how many days in each point, .... any tip that could help will be more than welcome!

Thanks a lot in advance SAP Community!

Best

entry.title: Re: Trip to USA east coast
entry.content: Hi @BrendanFarthing sounds very good! Thanks a lot for your feedback!

entry.title: SAP BTP Customer Forum Madrid 2023
entry.content:
Hola

El próximo 14 de Marzo se celebra el SAP BTP Customer Forum en Madrid.

Será una oportunidad para conectar con  clientes y partners, y crear comunidad al respecto de SAP BTP.

Durante la mañana habrá sesiones con AUSAPE (😉) , y keynotes con Irfan Khan (Chief Product Officer, HANA Database & Analytics) y Ragunath Ramanathan (Chief Revenue Officer BTP) además de sesiones de partners y clientes.

Por la tarde habrá dos tracks paralelos sobre gestión y analitica de datos y sobre integración y desarrollo de aplicaciones. En ambos tracks se explicará los últimos lanzamientos de productos SAP donde los mejores expertos explicarán el impacto relevante que estas últimas novedades pueden aportar a los landscapes SAP.

¿Nos vemos en Madrid?

Event link

https://events.sap.com/es/2023-03-14-sap-ie-btp-customer-forum-madrid/es/home

entry.title: Where are you from BCN Group members? What part of the city, or region are you from?
entry.content:
Hello BCN Group members!

Just to open a discussion to get to know each other a little better, so we launch this first question:

Where are you from? From Barcelona city? Other cities around Barcelona?

I will start to engage answering that I was born in Barcelona and currently my HQ Offices are also

in Barcelona, near Art de Triomf, but I live in Sant Cugat del Valles.

 

So, what about you?

😉

#CommunitySpirit

entry.title: Next SAP Stammtisch Barcelona is about BlockChain Technology - March 9th, 2023
entry.content:
Hi

Next Thursday March 9th we will have Mr Lloyd Keays (Incubation and Pioneering Executive, SAP Business Network)  to talkt about BlockChain ,the disrupting technology

A blockchain is a decentralized digital record of transactions shared across a network that’s immutable or unchangeable. It uses something called distributed ledger technology.

 

Will you join us?

We will be here at #discord at:

https://discord.gg/Jz6XphZf

Best

Lluis, Enric, Imma and David

entry.title: SAP Stammtisch Barcelona - BlockChain
entry.content:
Brought to you by SAP Community members, the SAP Stammtisch is an informal meetup everyone can participate: SAP users, developers, consultants and architects - come and join us and bring a friend. This time it's a virtual event.

Please find more information here at Barcelona Group:

https://groups.community.sap.com/t5/barcelona-discussions/next-sap-stammtisch-barcelona-is-about-blockchain-technology/m-p/223155

entry.title: SUGEN ~ SIG Leads Event 2023 ~ BTP Topic
entry.content:
SUGEN ~ SIG Leads Event 2023

Last March 21-22, a joint meeting of Special Interest Groups Leaders from eight countries was held at the SAP offices in St. Rot, Germany.
The purpose of the meeting was to hear from SAP about the latest developments in each of the areas, as well as to improve coordination and collaboration with SAP and between the different SIGs.

BTP Group

The BTP session was led by Arne Speck (Product Manager for SAP Business Technology Platform), who conducted the first part of the session.
In the first part of the session latest updates regarding the different components of SAP BTP stack were presented. The area that took the most time for explanation and comments was the integration component, given the importance of this topic within the Groups.

Afterwards, emphasis was placed on the message about the SAP BTP Unified Runtime strategy, a strategy to streamline the experience how to develop, build, deploy and operate applications across all multi-cloud environments. First message was that SAP BTP Neo is no longer a strategic stack for SAP, while Cloud Foundry is and will be. With this, the main message emphasized that SAP's strategy is that BTP services will be multicloud and can be consumed from any of the strategic runtimes (which are: Kyma based on Kubernetes, Cloud Foundry and ABAP Runtimes) and on top of any Cloud infrastructure services operator (hyperscalers).


The second part of the session was led by Manuel Namyslo (Solution Manager for SAP Build Process Automation). He conducted an interesting practical session on the three pillars of the services offered by SAP Build in BTP, which are SAP Build Apps (no-code solutions), SAP Build Process Automation and SAP Build Workzone.

In summary, there were 2 intensive days where networking with colleagues from other User Groups and SAP was the most interesting part.

entry.title: SAP SAPPHIRE 2023 Barcelona - Locals
entry.content:
Hi Barcelona SAP Community!!

Just would like to ask you if anyone  is attending SAP SAPPHIRE Barcelona next 2023 May 24th and 25th?

Please let me know, we have some ideas ...😊

Best regards,

David

entry.title: SAP Stammtisch Barcelona - May 11, 2023
entry.content:
[english follows]

SAP #StammtischBCN presencial en  Barcelona!
Te esperamos este próximo Jueves 11 de Mayo para un afterwork abierto e informal con la comunidad SAP de Barcelona. 
Para ello quedaremos en "Michael Collins"(Pub Irlandés),Plaça Sagrada Família, 4, 08013 Barcelona  20:00h 🇮🇪 🍻
¿Te apuntas? Nos vemos allí 

Dinos si podrás venir, haciendo "click"  en "I will attend", así podremos calcular las mesas necesarias. Más información sobre blogs en : Barcelona-blog-posts y te invitamos a que te unas al Grupo de Barcelona: join-Barcelona 

[english]

Hey!  Next SAP #StammtischBCN  will be in person !
So, everybody is invited to join next  Thursday 2023 May 11th  at 20:00h, to this informal meeting open to all SAP Community of Barcelona.
We will meet here  "Michael Collins"  Plaça Sagrada Família, 4, 08013 Barcelona, 20:00h 🇮🇪  🍻
Will you join us? 

Please let us know if you will participate in SAP Stammtisch by clicking "I will attend", which will allow us to reserve the right number of places at our meeting point.
Find more information in the blog posts board: Barcelona-blog-posts and we also invite you join the Barcelona Group: join-Barcelona 


#sapcommunity #sapstammtisch #sapstammtischbarcelona #sapbarcelona #usergroups

entry.title: SAP Stammtisch Barcelona, May 11, 2023 / Call for attendees
entry.content:
Hi

Please take a look here to get details for next SAP Stammtisch Barcelona

https://groups.community.sap.com/t5/sap-stammtisch/sap-stammtisch-barcelona-may-11-2023/ev-p/259049

Hope to see you next Thursday!

David

entry.title: Re: SAP Stammtisch Barcelona, May 11, 2023 / Call for attendees
entry.content: Hi @TammyPowlas , you know you are more than welcome here! 😊

entry.title: Re: SAP Stammtisch Barcelona, May 11, 2023 / Call for attendees
entry.content: Yes, @sveabecker , let's hope TechEd will come back to Barcelona again in the near future! 🤞

entry.title: SAP Inside Track Barcelona 2023 + SAP Community "Watch-and-code" party - Nov 2nd-3rd, 2023
entry.content:
Hi Barcelona!!

We are very excited to announce to you that SAP Inside Track Barcelona 7th edition will take place next November 2nd and 3rd 2023.

NEW!!!  This year we will also host "SAP Community "watch-and-code" party, which will take place in the morning of November 3rd . 

A SAP Inside Track (SIT) is a local grassroots community organised event where SAP Community Network members come together to share knowledge and expertise and to network about SAP related topics. SAP Inside Tracks have been very successful in countries all over the World.

Come to learn and share your knowledge with your peers as well as make new friends in an informal atmosphere.

SAP Inside Track events offer Community provided sessions from SAP Experts and passionate SCN members.

We invite you to participate and help us to do a great event.  Don’t forget to invite your colleagues.

When

SAP Inside Track event will take place in Barcelona next November 2nd and 3rd,  2023.

Where (NEW)

Thursday Nov 2nd - OFICINAS SYNTAX, (c/Lluçanès 38, 08022 Barcelona)

https://goo.gl/maps/N9iboXDmb6LyAttv9

Friday Nov 3rd -  Enel/Endesa  HQ Barcelona, Modernist Room (Carrer de Roger de Flor 48, 08018 Barcelona)

https://goo.gl/maps/Kp25w8mZgLo

 

 Meeting provided by

 

Who

For local and global SAP community (customers, consultants, freelancers, startups, press and bloggers, SAP employees) plus for those totally new to SAP. The most important is that you want to share and gain some knowledge and new buddies.

Registration

https://www.eventbrite.es/e/sap-inside-track-barcelona-incl-sap-community-watch-and-code-party-2023-tickets-693987444127

Sessions and Agenda (NEW)

This year 2023, agenda (*) will have next schema

1- Thursday Nov 2nd, afternoon, SITBarcelona Opening and Welcome.  and sessions (Block 1).

2- Afterwards, altogether dinner (TBC).

3- Friday Nov 3rd,

  • SAP Community "watch-and-code" party
    • Welcome and introduction to SAP Community "Watch-and-Code" Party
    • Keynote (full) or Keynote Highlight (30 min) + Expert Q&A Session
    • Break/beverages & snacks
    • Mini Code JAM: Extending SAP S/4HANA via SAP BTP (CAP & RAP)
    • Lunch
    • Engagement Activity & Closing SAP Community Watch & Code Party
  • SITBarcelona sessions (Block 2)
  • Closing SITBarcelona

* A detailed agenda will be published soon

Call for speakers and content is OPEN ==> LINK to summit sessions

Twitter

Official twitter account will be : @sitbarcelona

The hashtag for this SAP Inside Track is #sitBCN

About complete agenda, sponsorship packages, accomodation and travel info, social event,etc.. take a look at SIT Barcelona site

https://sitbarcelona.com

Hope to meet all of you there!

David Ruiz Badia, Lluis Salvador Suarez , Enric Castella, Imma Salmeron, Losel Tenzin & Mario de Felipe.

entry.title: Re: SAP Community "watch-and-code" parties
entry.content:
Hi all

Just to share with all of you that we will organize our next SITBarcelona 2023 next November 2nd and 3rd, and this year also will host SAP Community watch-and-code party (Nov 3rd, morning)

So, you are all invited to check details here:

https://groups.community.sap.com/t5/barcelona-blog-posts/sap-inside-track-barcelona-2023-sap-communi...

And of course, we will be more than happy if you would like to join us as speakers or attendees!!

Best, David

entry.title: SAP Inside Track Barcelona 2023 + SAP Community watch-and-code party
entry.content:
We are very excited to announce to you that SAP Inside Track Barcelona 7th edition

will take place next November 2nd and 3rd, 2023.

NEW!!!  This year we will also host "SAP Community "watch-and-code" party,

which will take place in the morning of November 3rd.

Call for speakers and content is OPEN ➡️ LINK to summit sessions

Costs: No costs, but registration is mandatory

Registration is open ➡️ LINK to register as attendee

Hope to meet you in Barcelona!

entry.title: SAP Inside Track Barcelona 2023 Recap
entry.content:
First time, SAP Inside Track Barcelona hosted also SAP Community watch-and-code meetup in parallel to what was happening in Bangalore, at SAP TechEd 2023. 

SAP Community Barcelona enjoyed 2 days event and experienced the transformative power of knowledge sharing and networking.

We would like to start our recap with a sincere gratituted to all who contributed making it happen, our attendees, speakers, sponsors and volunteers. This year with 70+ attendees that made  SITBarcelona gathering incredible.

On Thursday Nov 2nd, an Opening Altogether Dinner kicked off at this year main sponsor offices, Syntax. Cool place to experience networking, meet old colleagues, making new connections and enjoying relaxed conversations when having awesome pizzas and drinks.

On Friday Nov 3rd morning SAP Community watch-and-code meetup took place in Endesa Offices, in Espai Endesa modernist room. This in-person community event was hosted concurrently with SAP TechEd,  providing an exceptional opportunity for SAP enthusiasts to come together, network, learn, and code! Participants were able to rewatch the SAP TechEd keynote together and take part in a Q&A session with experts coming from SAP Spain. After this first block, attendees participated in the hands-on mini code jam session focused on extending SAP S/4HANA via SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). Here we would like to thank Enric Castella, Losel Tenzin , Xavier González and Sergio Martinez who volunteer as instructors helping to solve issues along the mini code jam session. Closing the morning block we made a raffle to win 4 e-books and 1-year free subscription thanks to Espresso Tutorials and SAP Press. Here we would like to thank so much our beloved SAP Champions and friend Nil Peksen who accepted to be on stage and run the draw. Congratulations to the winners:

  • Monica Corella
  • Kashish Arora
  • Gregory Doukas
  • Antonio Valladares
  • Ana Lopera

After recovering forces at lunch sponsored by SAP Community and AUSAPE, SITBarcelona sessions took place in two rooms:

Magdalena MurtraSAP independ Consultant Ecosystem. Get an overview of the SAP freelance consultant ecosystem
Antonio Ancos"Walldorf, tenemos un problema", hablando de la necesidad de formarse (cómo y dónde hacerlo) y de reaprender
Anxo CampoSAP & IA - Transforming Data Insights: The Synergy of self-hosted LLM's and SAP Hana
Cesar Martin & Luis AguadoABAP’s future in the Cloud
Alessandro SabidussiAI and ML scenario for SAP S/4HANA Cloud (public and private) - what's available and what's coming
Antonio ValladaresBest practices on Cloud Integration
Felipe Geva UrbanoShow de EHR from Hospital Clinic developed on ui5
Bernhard LueckeSAP4ME & Anything else you always wanted to ask about SAP Support 
Gregory DoukasStreamlining Complex Scenarios in SAP BTP
Fran VillegasInnovating with the AWS SDK for SAP ABAP
Luis Felipe Lanz & Diego PeregoSAP BTP Future Ready Enterprise, a solid framework for platform adoption
Felipe BorgesGlobal e-Invoicing and e-Reporting with SAP Document and Reporting Compliance

Remember that all content was recorded and now availabe here.  In a few weeks, also sessions slides will be available at SITBarcelona site.

As said before, big thanks to all our sponsors who year after year believe and support our SAP Community event:

 

Last but not least, we would to invite to all of you to join the SAP Community Barcelona Group, our group at SAP Community, it is our objective to make it our regular meeting point to share SAP related information Needless to say, the group is open to anyone! So, help us grow by sharing it with all your SAP contacts.

 

And final thanks to the amazing volunteers team who is behind SITBarcelona:

Lluis Salvador, Enric Castella, Losel Tenzin, Imma Salmerón, Mario de Felipe and me.

Behind the scenes, many thanks to Mireia Bubí, Laia Ruiz, Tamara Curiel!

And from SAP Community, big thanks to Laura Schmitz  and Svea Becker

 

See you next year!

entry.title: Re: SAP Inside Track Barcelona 2023 + SAP Community "Watch-and-code" party - Nov 2nd-3rd,
entry.content: Hi @sveabecker @gerardminari   Blog is already published in Barcelona Group. Sorry for the delay 😊

entry.title: SAP TechEd on Tour
entry.content:
Hi 

When will be announced the cities that will host next SAP TechEd on Tour this fall 2024?

(SAP Barcelona community will be more than happy to (re)welcome all of you 🤗 )

Thanks

Best, David
